questions.question: During the Drop-Tune Manoeuvre, what is the exact minimum time required for the contact-exclusion field to clear after releasing an object, and how is this rhythm trained?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 0.5 seconds, trained using a digital stopwatch cue

choices.label: B
choices.text: 0.3 seconds, trained using a metronome cue

choices.label: C
choices.text: 1.2 seconds, trained using a visual strobe cue

choices.label: D
choices.text: 1.0 seconds, trained using an auditory alarm cue

questions.explanation: Section 5.5 states that after releasing an object, the contact-exclusion field clears after ~0.3 seconds, and this exact 0.3 s minimum is trained using a metronome cue in Cell Halcón training videos.

questions.question: When a violation of the Three-Two-One Rule occurs, who triggers the automatic call-off, how is it monitored, and what has been the historical outcome of these documented violations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sgt. Quispe-Linares via physical site survey telemetry; 5 out of 7 violations produced operational anomalies.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Lt. Bauer-Cortez via Reset Bracer telemetry; 5 out of 7 violations produced operational anomalies.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Lt. Bauer-Cortez via Yatiri-3 burst comms; all 7 violations produced operational anomalies.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cpl. Vendrame via metabolic prep telemetry; 3 out of 5 violations produced operational anomalies.

questions.explanation: Section 5.2 states that violations of the Three-Two-One Rule trigger an automatic call-off by the cell handler, Lt. Bauer-Cortez, who monitors via Reset Bracer telemetry. Out of 7 documented violations, 5 produced operational anomalies.

questions.question: According to the core principles of the Doctrine of Sequential Suspension (DSS), what specific pattern defines the sequence of lethality, and what is the rule regarding withdrawal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ordered chains of two; withdraw after four activations and reset.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ordered chains of three; withdraw after three activations and reset.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ordered chains of four; withdraw after two activations and reset.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Continuous activations; withdraw only at 80% targeting fatigue.

questions.explanation: Section 5.1 outlines the core principles of the DSS: Principle 2 states that 'ordered chains of three define the doctrine', and Principle 6 states to 'Withdraw before exhaustion. Three activations and reset.'

questions.question: What operational advantage does the Drop-Tune Manoeuvre provide once the contact-exclusion field clears and the asset suspends the released object?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It allows a 1.2-second targeting window to protect the asset's acquisition vulnerability.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It permits a meaningful one-second window between release and engagement target acquisition.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It extends the Hold duration of the object up to 4 seconds.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It increases the maximum simultaneously held objects in the Suspended Field to 4.

questions.explanation: Section 5.5 states that the Drop-Tune technique 'permits a meaningful one-second window between release and engagement target acquisition.'

questions.question: Under the Tier-Based Engagement Protocols, which PAMC Threat Tier strictly enforces the Three-Two-One Rule?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Tier 2

choices.label: B
choices.text: Tier 3

choices.label: C
choices.text: Tier 4

choices.label: D
choices.text: Tier 5

questions.explanation: According to the table in Section 5.3, Threat Tier 3 requires a cell, lead authority, and has 'Three-Two-One enforced strictly' as its standing rule.

questions.question: Under what specific conditions is the asset permitted to access the Salar de Uyuni Sector 4 in Bolivia?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Access is completely forbidden under all circumstances due to pending bilateral agreements.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Limited access is permitted, requiring prior coordination due to an ongoing classified PAMC investigation.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Access is unrestricted, provided the asset is accompanied by Cpl. Vendrame during semi-annual audits.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Access is permitted only under direct authorization during extra-territorial deployments.

questions.explanation: According to Section 8.6, Salar de Uyuni Sector 4 is restricted due to a strategic, ongoing classified PAMC investigation since 2023, and its status is 'Limited access, prior coordination required.'

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the provisioning standards and audit schedule for the asset's five operational safehouses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 15-day rations for 2 personnel, audited quarterly by Cell Halcón.

choices.label: B
choices.text: 30-day rations for 4 personnel, audited semi-annually by Cpl. Vendrame.

choices.label: C
choices.text: 45-day rations for 4 personnel, audited annually by the Director-General.

choices.label: D
choices.text: 60-day rations for 6 personnel, audited biennially by Operative MIRADOR.

questions.explanation: Section 8.3 states that each safehouse maintains a standard provisioning of 30-day rations for 4 personnel, and safehouse provisioning is audited semi-annually by Cpl. Vendrame on behalf of the cell.

questions.question: What redundancy requirement must be met for any cell-led operation within the asset's operational territory regarding the PAMC Yatiri network?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Direct line-of-comms to all 8 burst-relay nodes simultaneously.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Continuous connection to the Valparaíso primary node and at least one backup satellite uplink.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Line-of-comms to at least 2 burst-relay nodes.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A primary connection to the closest node and a secondary line to the nearest micro-cache.

questions.explanation: Section 8.7 states that node redundancy requires each cell-led operation to have a line-of-comms to at least 2 nodes.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the secondary base located at Edificio Avenida Salaverry 1840 in Lima?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It serves as a logistics-only annex for southern operations.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It acts as a field office dedicated to Bolivian altiplano operations.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It serves as the PAMC Headquarters, where the asset rotates through quarterly for senior briefings.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It acts as a regional micro-lab for CBMP medical research and storage.

questions.explanation: Section 8.2 lists the function of the Lima secondary base (Edificio Avenida Salaverry 1840) as 'PAMC Headquarters; the asset rotates through quarterly for senior briefings.'

questions.question: Why is the Cerro Castillo exclusion ring restricted, and what is the asset's history with this zone?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is restricted due to jurisdictional disputes, and the asset has never been permitted to enter.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is restricted for psychological reasons based on a CBMP recommendation, and the asset has not entered since 2020.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is restricted due to an active EMP hazard, and the asset last accessed it in 2024.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is restricted for strategic investigation reasons, requiring prior coordination since 2023.

questions.explanation: Section 8.6 states that the Cerro Castillo exclusion ring (1.6 km radius around the 2018 trigger site) is restricted due to 'Psychological; CBMP recommendation' and that 'The asset has not entered since 2020 since the 2018 incident.'
